LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT EFFECTIVELY CONCEDES THAT PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL MAY SATISFY THE WRONGFUL CONDUCT ELEMENT OF INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE. DEFENDANT'S OTHER ARGUMENTS INVOLVE FACTUAL ISSUES NOT SUSCEPTIBLE TO DEMURRER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 =302/RU

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION WILL BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501, THE REAL PROPERTY AND HOUSING COURT, ON MARCH 16, 2018 AT 9:30AM. ALL FURTHER LAW AND MOTION MATTERS IN THIS CASE MUST BE NOTICED TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/HK)
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTIONF FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-16-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF PANKOWSKI'S SINGLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE FAILS BECAUSE THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT APPLICATION OF THE DOCTRINE OF PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL TO THE CITY'S ACTIONS TO AVOID INJUSTICE TO PLAINTIFF OUTWEIGHS THE SEVERE ADVERSE IMPACTS ON PUBLIC POLICIES PROMOTED BY THE CITY'S PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS FOR CONTRACTING TO BE IN WRITING AND IN A FORM APPROVED BY THE CITY ATTORNEY. PLAINTIFF WAS ACTING ON THE ORAL AND WRITTEN REPRESENTATION OF A PUBLIC OFFICIAL, NO ESTABLISHED PROCEDURE WAS FOLLOWED, AND THE CITY'S FAILURE TO HONOR THE PROMISE MADE IN THE REPRESENTATION DOES NOT DEVIATE FROM ANY APPROVED STANDARDS OR PROCEDURES, AND THE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUFFERED THE SUBSTANTIAL PRIVATE HARDSHIP. SEE SCHAFER V. CITY OF LOS ANGELES (2015) 237 CAL.APP.4TH 1250, 1263; STATE OF CALIFORNIA V. HASLETT CO. (1975) 45 CAL.APP.3D 252, 257; HPT IHG-2 PROPERTIES TRUST V. CITY OF ANAHEIM (2015) 243 CAL.APP.4TH 188. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2018 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Mar-22-2018 Set For Case Management Conference On Jun-27-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sandra Lowenstein (925-279-3463) for Plaintiff/ opposing party Ronald Pankowski.
Hunter Sims, III (415-554-4259) for Defendant/Moving party CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

Hearing is held. The Court annouces the tentative ruling. Defendant submits to the tentative ruling. Argued by Plaintiff re: estoppel, unique set of facts/circumstances, severe adverse impact on public policy, balance of equity and city benefit. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted. Plaintiff Pankowski's single cause of action for intentional interference fails because the court does not find that application of the doctrine of promissory estoppel to the city's actions to avoid injustice to plaintiff outweighs the severe adverse impacts on public policies promoted by the City's procedural requirements for contracting to be in writing and in a form approved by the city attorney. Plaintiff was acting on the oral and written representation of a public official, no established procedure was followed, and the city's failure to honor the promise made in the representation does not deviate from any approved standards or procedures, and the plaintiff has not suffered the substantial private hardship. See Schafer v. City of Los Angeles (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1250, 1263; State of California v. Haslett Co. (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 252, 257; HPT IHG-2 Properties Trust v. City of Anaheim (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th 188. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer is OVERRULED. Defendant effectively concedes that promissory estoppel may satisfy the wrongful conduct element of intentional interference with prospective economic advantage. Defendant's other arguments involve factual issues not susceptible to demurrer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ru

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-30-2016 Continued To Sep-07-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hunter Sims of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4259 for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of oral contract and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage. Plaintiff Ronald Pankowski's claim for breach of oral contract is not cognizable due to the City Charter provision requiring that the contract be in writing. (Katsura v. City of San Buenaventura (2007) 155 Ca. App. 4th 104). The claim for intentional interference fails because the alleged interference consisted of "false misrepresentations and false promises" and Government Code 818.8 precludes liability against a public entity for injuries caused by misrepresentations by its employees. (Complaint paragraph 36). Because the opposition memorandum suggests that Mr. Pankowski may be able to allege a different basis for an intentional interference claim, Mr. Pankowski is given leave to do so. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)
